text: The work done by a torque \(\tau\) through angular displacement \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Rotational work: \( W = \tau\theta \). Analogous to linear work \( W = Fs \).
name: Work by Torque
subject: phy

text: Inductive effect (–I) is the permanent displacement of σ-bond electrons due to electronegativity difference. Which group shows +I effect?
explanation: +I effect (electron-donating by induction): alkyl groups (–CH₃, –C₂H₅). –I effect (electron-withdrawing): –NO₂, –COOH, –Cl, –OH.
name: Inductive Effect
subject: che

text: If \( P(A) = 0.4 \) and \( P(B) = 0.5 \) and \( A, B \) are mutually exclusive, then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
explanation: Mutually exclusive: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A)+P(B) = 0.4+0.5 = 0.9 \)
name: Mutually Exclusive Events
subject: maths

text: The slope of the \(x\)-axis is:
explanation: The \(x\)-axis is horizontal: slope \( = 0 \). The \(y\)-axis is vertical: slope undefined (\(\infty\)).
name: Slope of Axes
subject: maths

text: In the reaction \( 2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \to 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \), 4 g of \( \text{H}_2 \) reacts with how many grams of \( \text{O}_2 \)?
explanation: 4 g \(\text{H}_2\) = 2 mol. From stoichiometry, 2 mol \(\text{H}_2\) needs 1 mol \(\text{O}_2\) = 32 g.
name: Stoichiometry Calculation
subject: che

text: The reaction quotient \(Q_c\) compared to \(K_c\): if \( Q_c < K_c \), the reaction will:
explanation: If \(Q < K\): reaction proceeds forward (products must increase). If \(Q > K\): reaction proceeds reverse.
name: Reaction Quotient Q
subject: che

text: \( \sin C + \sin D = \)
explanation: Sum-to-Product: \( \sin C + \sin D = 2\sin\frac{C+D}{2}\cos\frac{C-D}{2} \)
name: Sum-to-Product Formula
subject: maths

text: The cross product \( \vec{a} \times \vec{b} \) gives a vector:
explanation: \( \vec{a}\times\vec{b} \) is perpendicular to both \(\vec{a}\) and \(\vec{b}\) (normal to their plane).
name: Cross Product Direction
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\,dx = \)
explanation: Standard result: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\,dx = \sin^{-1}x+C \)
name: Arcsin Integral
subject: maths

text: The difference of focal distances from any point on hyperbola \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: For any point on hyperbola: \( |PF_1 - PF_2| = 2a \) (constant). This defines the hyperbola.
name: Focal Distance Difference
subject: maths

text: For hyperbola \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \), the asymptotes are:
explanation: Asymptotes of hyperbola: \( y = \pm\frac{b}{a}x \) (pass through center).
name: Asymptotes of Hyperbola
subject: maths

text: Fehling's solution is reduced by:
explanation: Fehling's test: gives brick-red Cu₂O precipitate with aliphatic aldehydes. Aromatic aldehydes (benzaldehyde) do NOT reduce Fehling's. Ketones: negative.
name: Fehling's Test
subject: che

text: The gravitational potential at a point is defined as:
options: Potential energy per unit mass: \( V = -GM/r \)
explanation: Gravitational potential: \( V = -\frac{GM}{r} \) (J/kg). Negative sign: potential energy of unit mass. \( U = mV \).
name: Gravitational Potential
subject: phy

text: The range of \( \tanh x \) is:
explanation: \( \tanh x \to \pm 1 \) as \( x \to \pm \infty \), but never equals \(\pm 1\). Range is \((-1,1)\).
name: Range of tanh
subject: maths

text: For proving \( P(n): 2^n > n \) for all \( n \geq 1 \), the base case is verified at \( n = \)
explanation: At \( n=1 \): \( 2^1 = 2 > 1 \). Base case holds.
name: Base Case Verification
subject: maths

text: For parametric equations \( x=f(t), y=g(t) \), \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \)
explanation: Parametric differentiation: \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{dy/dt}{dx/dt} = \frac{g'(t)}{f'(t)} \)
name: Parametric Differentiation
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}\,dx = \)
explanation: Standard formula: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}dx = \sin^{-1}\frac{x}{a}+C \)
name: Arcsin Standard Form
subject: maths

text: Walli's formula: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\sin^m x\cos^n x\,dx \) involves the pattern of multiplying down by \(2\) at the end when both \(m\) and \(n\) are:
explanation: When both \(m\) and \(n\) are even, Walli's formula requires an additional factor of \(\frac{\pi}{2}\).
name: Walli's Formula
subject: maths

text: The Poynting vector \(\vec{S}\) represents:
options: Energy flux (power per unit area) of EM wave: \( \vec{S} = \frac{1}{\mu_0}\vec{E}\times\vec{B} \)
explanation: Poynting vector: \( \vec{S} = \frac{\vec{E}\times\vec{B}}{\mu_0} \). Magnitude = intensity (W/m²). Direction = energy flow direction (same as wave propagation).
name: Poynting Vector
subject: phy

text: The wavelength of the spectral line in hydrogen spectrum is given by Rydberg formula:
explanation: Rydberg formula: \( \bar{\nu} = \frac{1}{\lambda} = R_H\left(\frac{1}{n_1^2}-\frac{1}{n_2^2}\right) \), \(R_H = 1.097\times10^7\,\text{m}^{-1}\).
name: Rydberg Formula
subject: che

text: The escape velocity from Earth is:
explanation: Escape velocity: \( v_e = \sqrt{\frac{2GM}{R}} = \sqrt{2gR} \approx 11.2\,\text{km/s} \). Note: \( v_e = \sqrt{2}\,v_o \).
name: Escape Velocity
subject: phy

text: If \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = 5 \) and \( \alpha\beta = 2 \), then \( (\alpha - \beta)^2 = \)
explanation: \( (\alpha-\beta)^2 = \alpha^2+\beta^2 - 2\alpha\beta = 5 - 4 = 1 \)
name: Difference of Roots Squared
subject: maths

text: The reaction of phenol with Br₂ water (without catalyst) gives:
explanation: Phenol is highly activated toward EAS. Br₂/water (no catalyst needed): gives 2,4,6-tribromophenol (white ppt). Used as test for phenol.
name: Bromination of Phenol
subject: che

text: The cyclotron frequency \(f_c\) (independent of speed) is:
explanation: Cyclotron frequency: \( f_c = \frac{qB}{2\pi m} \). Independent of speed (radius increases but period stays same). Maximum KE limited by relativistic effects.
name: Cyclotron Frequency
subject: phy

text: The quantity of electricity required to deposit 1 mole of Cu (2+ ion) is:
explanation: \( \text{Cu}^{2+} + 2e^- \to \text{Cu} \). 2 moles of electrons needed = 2F = \( 2 \times 96500 = 193000 \) C.
name: Charge to Deposit Cu
subject: che

text: The ILATE rule for integration by parts orders: I=Inverse trig, L=Log, A=Algebraic, T=Trig, E=Exponential. The function to choose as \(u\) is:
options: The one that comes later in ILATE
The one that comes first in ILATE
explanation: In ILATE, choose \(u\) as the function that comes first: Inverse trig > Log > Algebraic > Trig > Exponential.
name: ILATE Rule
subject: maths

text: Transition elements are defined as elements that have:
options: Partially filled d orbitals in ground state or common oxidation state
explanation: Transition elements: partially filled d orbitals in ground state OR any common oxidation state. Zn, Cd, Hg are border cases.
name: Definition of Transition Elements
subject: che

text: \( \cos 3\theta = \)
explanation: Triple angle formula: \( \cos 3\theta = 4\cos^3\theta - 3\cos\theta \)
name: Triple Angle - cos
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - 3x + 2 = 0 \), then \( \frac{1}{\alpha} + \frac{1}{\beta} = \)
explanation: \( \frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\beta} = \frac{\alpha+\beta}{\alpha\beta} = \frac{3}{2} \)
name: Sum of Reciprocals
subject: maths

text: The electron in hydrogen at n=2 has energy:
name: Energy at n=2
subject: phy

text: \( \frac{A}{x-2} = 3 \) for all \( x \) means \( A = \)
explanation: \( \frac{A}{x-2} = 3 \Rightarrow A = 3(x-2) \). This illustrates that \(A\) must be found by comparing coefficients or using specific \(x\) values.
name: Constant Determination
subject: maths

text: In a galvanic cell, oxidation occurs at:
explanation: In galvanic cell: Oxidation at anode (negative electrode), Reduction at cathode (positive electrode). Mnemonic: AnOx RedCat.
name: Oxidation at Anode
subject: che

text: The reaction of aldehyde with ammonia derivatives (NH₂–G) giving C=N compounds is:
explanation: R–CHO + H₂N–G → R–CH=N–G + H₂O. Two steps: (1) nucleophilic addition to give carbinolamine, (2) dehydration. Net: condensation.
name: Nucleophilic Addition-Elimination
subject: che

text: Choke coil (used to control AC without power loss) has:
options: Low resistance, high inductance (high reactance, low power factor)
explanation: Choke coil: high \(L\), low \(R\). High \(X_L = \omega L\) limits AC current with minimal heat loss. Power factor ≈ 0, very efficient AC current controller.
name: Choke Coil
subject: phy

text: The general electronic configuration of transition elements (3d series) is:
explanation: 3d transition series: [Ar] 3d¹⁻¹⁰ 4s¹⁻². Exceptions: Cr ([Ar]3d⁵4s¹) and Cu ([Ar]3d¹⁰4s¹) due to extra stability of half-filled/fully-filled d.
name: Electronic Config of Transition Elements
subject: che

text: Principal solution of \( \sin\theta = \frac{1}{2} \) in \( [0, 2\pi] \) are:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \frac{1}{2} \) in \([0,2\pi]\): \( \theta = \frac{\pi}{6} \) (1st quad) and \( \pi - \frac{\pi}{6} = \frac{5\pi}{6} \) (2nd quad).
name: Principal Solutions
subject: maths

text: The distance between points \( (x_1,y_1,z_1) \) and \( (x_2,y_2,z_2) \) in 3D is:
explanation: 3D Distance Formula: \( d = \sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2+(y_2-y_1)^2+(z_2-z_1)^2} \)
name: 3D Distance Formula
subject: maths

text: Aldehydes and ketones are distinguished by Tollens' reagent (ammoniacal AgNO₃). Aldehydes give:
explanation: Tollens' test: RCHO + 2[Ag(NH₃)₂]⁺ + 2OH⁻ → RCOOH + 2Ag↓ + 4NH₃. Silver mirror formed. Ketones give no reaction.
name: Tollens' Test
subject: che

text: The capacitive reactance \(X_C\) is:
explanation: Capacitive reactance: \( X_C = 1/\omega C \). Decreases with frequency. Leads voltage — current leads voltage by 90°.
name: Capacitive Reactance
subject: phy

text: Which reagent is used to oxidise primary alcohol to aldehyde without over-oxidising to carboxylic acid?
explanation: PCC (CH₂Cl₂, mild): oxidises 1° alcohol → aldehyde (stops there). Strong oxidants (KMnO₄, K₂Cr₂O₇) → carboxylic acid.
name: Selective Oxidation to Aldehyde
subject: che

text: Optical fibre communication works on the principle of:
options: Total internal reflection (light confined inside fibre core)
explanation: Optical fibre: light undergoes TIR inside core (high n) surrounded by cladding (lower n). Very high bandwidth, low loss, immune to EM interference.
name: Optical Fibre Principle
subject: phy

text: The number of significant figures in 0.00450 is:
explanation: Leading zeros are not significant. In 0.00450: 4, 5, 0 (trailing zero after decimal) = 3 significant figures.
name: Significant Figures
subject: phy

text: The transistor in common emitter configuration has:
explanation: CE configuration: \( \beta = I_C/I_B \gg 1 \). Both current gain and voltage gain > 1 → power amplification. Most used transistor configuration.
name: Common Emitter Current Gain
subject: phy

text: Geometric isomerism (cis-trans) is possible in square planar complexes of type:
explanation: cis-trans isomers in square planar: MA₂B₂ type. E.g., [Pt(NH₃)₂Cl₂]: cis (cisplatin, anticancer) and trans isomers.
name: Geometric Isomerism
subject: che

text: The focus of parabola \( y^2 = 4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2 = 4ax \): vertex at origin, focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \(x=-a\).
name: Focus of Parabola
subject: maths

text: Aryl halides are less reactive than alkyl halides toward nucleophilic substitution because:
options: C–X bond is stronger in aryl halides
Resonance makes C–X bond shorter and stronger (partial double bond character)
explanation: In aryl halides, lone pairs on X overlap with aromatic π system → C–X has partial double bond character → C–X bond shorter/stronger → harder to break.
name: Aryl Halide Reactivity
subject: che

text: Rolle's theorem requires \(f\) to be continuous on \([a,b]\), differentiable on \((a,b)\), and:
explanation: Rolle's Theorem: if additionally \( f(a) = f(b) \), then \( \exists\, c\in(a,b) \) such that \( f'(c)=0 \).
name: Rolle's Theorem Condition
subject: maths

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\tan x}{x} = \)
explanation: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\tan x}{x} = \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x}\cdot\frac{1}{\cos x} = 1\cdot1 = 1 \)
name: tan x / x Limit
subject: maths

text: A sigma (σ) bond is formed by:
options: Overlap of d and p orbitals only
explanation: σ bond: end-to-end (head-on) overlap along internuclear axis. π bond: lateral (sideways) overlap.
name: Sigma Bond Formation
subject: che

text: A Bernoulli DE has the form:
explanation: Bernoulli's equation: \( \frac{dy}{dx}+P(x)y=Q(x)y^n \). Reduce by substituting \(v=y^{1-n}\).
name: Bernoulli's Equation
subject: maths

text: Number of ways to arrange 5 books on a shelf is:
explanation: Arrangements of 5 distinct items \( = 5! = 120 \)
name: Arrangements
subject: maths

text: The reaction of alkanes with Cl₂ in UV light is:
explanation: Halogenation of alkanes: free radical substitution. Initiation: Cl₂ → 2Cl• (UV). Propagation: Cl• + CH₄ → CH₃• + HCl, then CH₃• + Cl₂ → CH₃Cl + Cl•.
name: Halogenation of Alkanes
subject: che

text: The equation of SHM \( \frac{d^2x}{dt^2} = -\omega^2 x \) shows that acceleration:
options: Is proportional to displacement, in same direction
Is proportional to displacement, in opposite direction (towards equilibrium)
explanation: SHM condition: \( a = -\omega^2 x \). Acceleration is always directed towards equilibrium (restoring force), proportional to displacement.
name: SHM Condition
subject: phy

explanation: Let \( \cos^{-1}x = \theta \Rightarrow \cos\theta = x \). Then \( \sin\theta = \sqrt{1-\cos^2\theta} = \sqrt{1-x^2} \).
name: sin(cos⁻¹x)
subject: maths

text: Number of 3-digit numbers formed from digits 1,2,3,4,5 (no repetition) is:
name: Digits without Repetition
subject: maths

text: If one root of \( x^2 - 5x + k = 0 \) is 2, then \( k = \)
explanation: Substituting \( x=2 \): \( 4 - 10 + k = 0 \Rightarrow k = 6 \)
name: Finding k from Root
subject: maths

text: The pair of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) coincide when:
explanation: Lines coincide (repeated) when \( h^2 = ab \) (discriminant = 0).
name: Coincident Lines
subject: maths

text: The formula for population variance is:
explanation: Population variance: \( \sigma^2 = \frac{\sum(x_i - \bar{x})^2}{n} \)
name: Variance Formula
subject: maths

text: A function \(f\) is continuous at \(x=a\) if:
explanation: Continuity at \(x=a\): (1) \(f(a)\) defined, (2) limit exists, (3) \( \lim_{x\to a}f(x)=f(a) \).
name: Continuity Definition
subject: maths

text: The equation \( a\sin\theta + b\cos\theta = c \) has real solutions if:
explanation: The equation has real solutions iff \( |c| \leq \sqrt{a^2+b^2} \), i.e., \( c^2 \leq a^2+b^2 \).
name: Condition for Real Solutions
subject: maths

text: Conservation of angular momentum: when a spinning ice skater pulls in arms, angular velocity:
options: Increases (I decreases, so ω increases to keep L constant)
explanation: \( L = I\omega = \text{const} \). Pulling arms in reduces \(I\), so \(\omega\) must increase. Classic example of angular momentum conservation.
name: Ice Skater Angular Momentum
subject: phy

text: Which molecule has zero dipole moment despite polar bonds?
explanation: \(\text{CO}_2\) is linear; two C=O dipoles cancel → net dipole = 0. \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\) and \(\text{NH}_3\) have net dipole.
name: Zero Dipole Moment
subject: che

text: The angle \(\theta\) between lines with direction cosines \( (l_1,m_1,n_1) \) and \( (l_2,m_2,n_2) \) is:
explanation: \( \cos\theta = l_1l_2+m_1m_2+n_1n_2 \). Lines are perpendicular when this equals 0.
name: Angle Between Lines in 3D
subject: maths

text: The combined equation \( ax^2 + 2hxy + by^2 = 0 \) represents a pair of lines through origin. The angle \(\theta\) between them satisfies:
explanation: Angle between pair of lines: \( \tan\theta = \frac{2\sqrt{h^2-ab}}{a+b} \)
name: Angle Between Pair of Lines
subject: maths

text: The magnetic flux \(\Phi_B\) through a loop of area \(A\) in field \(B\) at angle \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Magnetic flux: \( \Phi_B = \vec{B}\cdot\vec{A} = BA\cos\theta \), where \(\theta\) is angle between \(\vec{B}\) and area normal \(\hat{n}\). Unit: Weber (Wb).
name: Magnetic Flux Formula
subject: phy

text: In the photoelectric effect, the saturation current depends on:
options: Intensity of incident light (more photons → more electrons)
explanation: Saturation current ∝ intensity (number of photons per second). Maximum KE depends on frequency, not intensity. Einstein's explanation.
name: Saturation Current and Intensity
subject: phy

text: In an equilateral triangle with side \(a\), the area is:
explanation: For equilateral triangle: \( \Delta = \frac{1}{2}a\cdot a\sin 60° = \frac{a^2\sqrt{3}}{4} \)
name: Equilateral Triangle Area
subject: maths

text: DDT (dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane) is:
explanation: DDT: chlorinated insecticide, non-biodegradable (persists in environment, accumulates in food chain). Banned in most countries now.
name: DDT
subject: che

text: Bulk modulus is defined as:
explanation: Bulk modulus: \( B = -\frac{\Delta P}{\Delta V/V} \). Negative sign because pressure increase causes volume decrease. Liquids/gases have only bulk modulus.
name: Bulk Modulus
subject: phy

text: If two roots of \( x^3 - px^2 + qx - r = 0 \) are equal, say \( \alpha, \alpha, \beta \), then which condition must hold?
explanation: From Vieta's: \( 2\alpha+\beta=p \), \( \alpha^2+2\alpha\beta=q \), \( \alpha^2\beta=r \). When all three equal: \( p^3=27r \). All conditions can hold simultaneously.
name: Equal Roots in Cubic
subject: maths

text: If \( f(x) \) is an even function, then \( \int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: Even function property: \( \int_{-a}^{a}f(x)dx = 2\int_0^a f(x)dx \)
name: Even Function Integral
subject: maths

text: In a right triangle with right angle at \(C\), then \( c^2 = \)
explanation: When \( C = 90°, \cos C = 0 \): \( c^2 = a^2 + b^2 - 2ab(0) = a^2 + b^2 \). Pythagorean theorem.
name: Pythagoras from Cosine Rule
subject: maths

text: The distance between points \( (x_1,y_1) \) and \( (x_2,y_2) \) is:
explanation: Distance Formula: \( d = \sqrt{(x_2-x_1)^2 + (y_2-y_1)^2} \)
name: Distance Formula
subject: maths

text: The impulse-momentum theorem states:
explanation: Impulse \( J = F\Delta t = \Delta p = mv - mu \). Explains why airbags (increase \(\Delta t\)) reduce force in collisions.
name: Impulse-Momentum Theorem
subject: phy

text: Two circles are orthogonal if:
explanation: Orthogonality condition: \( 2g_1g_2+2f_1f_2 = c_1+c_2 \)
name: Orthogonal Circles
subject: maths

text: A body will float in a liquid if:
options: Its density is greater than liquid density
Its weight equals or is less than buoyant force (density ≤ liquid density)
explanation: Floating condition: \( W \leq F_b \), i.e., \( \rho_{body} \leq \rho_{liquid} \). If \(\rho_{body} = \rho_{liquid}\): body submerged but in equilibrium.
name: Floating Condition
subject: phy

text: Joule's law of heating states heat produced \( H = \)
explanation: Joule's law: \( H = I^2Rt \) (in joules). In calories: \( H = I^2Rt/4.18 \). Basis for electric heaters, fuses.
name: Joule's Law of Heating
subject: phy

text: In an adiabatic process:
explanation: Adiabatic: thermally insulated system, \( Q = 0 \). Work done by gas comes from internal energy: \( W = -\Delta U \).
name: Adiabatic Process
subject: phy

text: For what value of \( k \) does \( x^2 - kx + 4 = 0 \) have equal roots?
explanation: Equal roots when \( D = 0 \Rightarrow k^2 - 16 = 0 \Rightarrow k = \pm 4 \)
name: Equal Roots Condition
subject: maths

text: Plaster of Paris is:
explanation: Plaster of Paris: \( \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} \) (hemi-hydrate). Sets by absorbing water: \( \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} + \frac{3}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O} \to \text{CaSO}_4 \cdot 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \).
name: Plaster of Paris
subject: che

text: The electric field on the axial line of a dipole at large distance \(r\) is:
explanation: Axial field: \( E_{axial} = \frac{2p}{4\pi\epsilon_0 r^3} \) (along dipole axis). Equatorial: \( E_{eq} = \frac{p}{4\pi\epsilon_0 r^3} \). Ratio = 2:1.
name: Dipole Axial Field
subject: phy

text: By induction, \( n^3 + 2n \) is divisible by:
explanation: \( n^3+2n = n(n^2+2) \). Among 3 consecutive integers, one is divisible by 3; this expression factors to always be divisible by 3.
name: Divisibility of n³+2n
subject: maths

text: The number of 4th roots of unity is:
explanation: The equation \( z^4 = 1 \) has exactly 4 roots: \( 1, -1, i, -i \)
name: Roots of Unity Count
subject: maths

text: The electric field \(\vec{E}\) at a point is defined as:
options: Force on any charge placed there
Force per unit positive test charge: \( \vec{E} = \vec{F}/q_0 \)
explanation: Electric field: \( \vec{E} = \vec{F}/q_0 \) (force per unit positive test charge). Units: N/C = V/m.
name: Definition of Electric Field
subject: phy

text: The domain of \( f(x) = \log(x^2 - x - 6) \) is:
explanation: Need \( x^2-x-6 > 0 \Rightarrow (x-3)(x+2) > 0 \Rightarrow x > 3 \) or \( x < -2 \)
name: Domain of Logarithm
subject: maths

text: The Quotient Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}\left(\frac{u}{v}\right) = \)
explanation: Quotient Rule: \( \left(\frac{u}{v}\right)' = \frac{u'v - uv'}{v^2} \)
name: Quotient Rule
subject: maths

text: Photochemical smog involves:
options: NOₓ + unburnt hydrocarbons + UV → O₃, PAN, aldehydes
explanation: Photochemical smog: NOₓ + HC (UV light) → O₃ + PAN (peroxyacetyl nitrate) + formaldehyde. Brown haze, causes eye/lung irritation.
name: Photochemical Smog
subject: che

text: Diagonal relationship exists between:
explanation: Diagonal relationship: Li-Mg, Be-Al, B-Si. Elements show similarity with the element diagonally below-right due to similar charge/size ratio.
name: Diagonal Relationship
subject: che

explanation: Set \( x=1 \): \( \frac{1+1}{1 \cdot 0+...} \to 2 = C \cdot \frac{1}{1} \Rightarrow C = 2 \)
name: Repeated Factor Constant
subject: maths

text: The time period of a torsional pendulum depends on:
options: Moment of inertia \(I\) and torsional constant \(C\): \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{I/C} \)
explanation: Torsional pendulum: \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{I/C} \), where \(I\) = moment of inertia, \(C\) = restoring torque per unit angle.
name: Torsional Pendulum Period
subject: phy

text: The value of \( i^{97} \) is:
explanation: \( i^{97} = i^{4 \times 24 + 1} = i^1 = i \). Powers of \(i\) cycle with period 4.
name: Powers of i
subject: maths

text: If 1 is a root of \( x^3 + ax^2 + bx - 2 = 0 \), then:
explanation: Substituting \(x=1\): \( 1 + a + b - 2 = 0 \Rightarrow a + b = 1 \)
name: Root Substitution Condition
subject: maths

text: Colligative properties depend on:
explanation: Colligative properties (VP lowering, BP elevation, FP depression, osmotic pressure) depend only on the number of solute particles.
name: Colligative Properties
subject: che

text: Electrophilic substitution (EAS) on benzene: the rate-determining step involves:
options: Loss of H⁺ from sigma complex
Addition of electrophile to all carbons
explanation: EAS mechanism: (1) Formation of π complex, (2) Formation of σ complex (arenium ion, slow, RDS), (3) Loss of H⁺ (fast) to restore aromaticity.
name: EAS Rate-Determining Step
subject: che

text: The value of \( \cos 60° = \)
explanation: \( \cos 60° = \frac{1}{2} \). Standard trigonometric value.
name: cos 60°
subject: maths

text: Using Binomial theorem, the approximate value of \( (1.01)^5 \) up to 3 decimal places is:
name: Binomial Approximation
subject: maths

text: Vitamins that are fat-soluble are:
explanation: Fat-soluble: A, D, E, K (stored in body fat and liver). Water-soluble: B-complex and C (must be taken regularly). Excess fat-soluble → toxicity possible.
name: Fat-Soluble Vitamins
subject: che

text: The value of \( (1+i)^8 \) is:
explanation: \( 1+i = \sqrt{2}e^{i\pi/4} \Rightarrow (1+i)^8 = (\sqrt{2})^8 e^{i2\pi} = 16 \cdot 1 = 16 \)
name: Powers using De-Moivre
subject: maths

text: According to VSEPR theory, the shape of \( \text{NH}_3 \) is:
explanation: \(\text{NH}_3\): 3 bond pairs + 1 lone pair → trigonal pyramidal shape (tetrahedral electron geometry).
name: Shape of NH₃
subject: che

text: The mirror formula is:
explanation: Mirror formula: \( \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{u} = \frac{1}{f} = \frac{2}{R} \). Sign convention: distances measured from pole; positive in direction of incident light.
name: Mirror Formula
subject: phy

text: The standard equation of a circle with center \((h,k)\) and radius \(r\) is:
explanation: Standard form: \( (x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2 \). When center is origin: \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \).
name: Standard Circle Equation
subject: maths

text: The photoelectric effect is explained by:
explanation: Einstein explained photoelectric effect using photon concept: \( KE = h\nu - h\nu_0 \) (photon energy - work function).
name: Photoelectric Effect
subject: che

text: The greatest binomial coefficient in \( (1+x)^{10} \) is:
explanation: For even \(n = 10\), greatest coefficient is the middle one: \( \binom{10}{5} = 252 \)
name: Greatest Binomial Coefficient
subject: maths

text: Heavy water (D₂O) differs from ordinary water in having:
options: Higher density and higher boiling point
Lower density and lower boiling point
explanation: D₂O: density 1.11 g/mL (vs 1.00 for H₂O), bp 101.4°C (vs 100°C), mp 3.8°C (vs 0°C). Heavier due to deuterium.
name: Heavy Water Properties
subject: che

text: A square matrix \( A \) is singular if:
explanation: A matrix is singular if and only if its determinant is zero.
name: Singular Matrix
subject: maths

text: A skew-symmetric matrix satisfies:
explanation: A matrix is skew-symmetric if \( A^T = -A \), i.e., \( A + A^T = 0 \).
name: Skew-Symmetric Matrix
subject: maths

text: \( \binom{n}{r} + \binom{n}{r-1} = \)
explanation: Pascal's Identity: \( \binom{n}{r} + \binom{n}{r-1} = \binom{n+1}{r} \)
name: Pascal's Identity
subject: maths

text: For a first-order reaction, the integrated rate law is:
explanation: First-order: \( \ln[A] = \ln[A]_0 - kt \) or \( [A] = [A]_0 e^{-kt} \).
name: First Order Integrated Rate Law
subject: che

text: The number of terms in the expansion of \( (a+b+c)^n \) is:
name: Trinomial Expansion Terms
subject: maths

text: The thermal resistance \(R_{th}\) of a slab of length \(L\), area \(A\), conductivity \(k\) is:
explanation: Thermal resistance: \( R_{th} = L/(kA) \). Analogous to electrical resistance. \( \dot{Q} = \Delta T / R_{th} \).
name: Thermal Resistance
subject: phy

text: The value of \( \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = \)
explanation: Pythagorean identity: \( \sin^2\theta + \cos^2\theta = 1 \) for all \(\theta\).
name: Pythagorean Identity
subject: maths

text: The work-energy theorem states:
options: Net work done on a body = change in kinetic energy
explanation: Work-Energy Theorem: \( W_{net} = \Delta KE = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 - \frac{1}{2}mu^2 \).
name: Work-Energy Theorem
subject: phy

text: When capacitors are connected in parallel, the equivalent capacitance is:
explanation: Parallel: \( C_{eq} = C_1 + C_2 + \cdots \) (capacitances add). Series: \( 1/C_{eq} = 1/C_1 + 1/C_2 + \cdots \).
name: Capacitors in Parallel
subject: phy

text: Melde's experiment demonstrates the formation of standing waves on a string. The number of loops \(p\) is related to tension \(T\) by:
explanation: Melde's experiment: \( p = \frac{L}{\lambda/2} \), frequency \( f = p\frac{v}{2L} = p\frac{1}{2L}\sqrt{T/\mu} \). So \( p \propto 1/\sqrt{T} \) at fixed \(f\).
name: Melde's Experiment
subject: phy

text: If \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{pmatrix} \), then \( |A| = \)
name: 2x2 Determinant
subject: maths

text: The maximum value of \( f(x) = \sin x + \cos x \) is:
explanation: \( f(x) = \sqrt{2}\sin(x+\frac{\pi}{4}) \). Maximum \( = \sqrt{2} \) when \( x = \frac{\pi}{4} \).
name: Maximum of sin+cos
subject: maths

text: Number of functions from a set of 3 elements to a set of 2 elements is:
explanation: Each of 3 elements can map to 2 choices. Total \( = 2^3 = 8 \)
name: Number of Functions
subject: maths

text: The displacement in the \(n\)th second of uniformly accelerated motion is:
explanation: \( s_n = u + \frac{a}{2}(2n-1) \). This is displacement in the nth second specifically.
name: Displacement in nth Second
subject: phy

text: A body moves with uniform speed in a circle. Its acceleration is:
explanation: Uniform circular motion: speed is constant but direction changes. Centripetal acceleration \( a = \frac{v^2}{r} \) directed towards centre.
name: Acceleration in Circular Motion
subject: phy

text: Thermoplastics differ from thermosetting plastics in that they:
options: Can be softened on heating and reshaped (linear/branched chains)
explanation: Thermoplastics: linear or branched → soften on heating, can be remoulded and recycled (PVC, polyethylene, nylon). Thermosets: cross-linked → cannot be remoulded.
name: Thermoplastics vs Thermosets
subject: che

text: The length of tangent from external point \((x_1,y_1)\) to circle \( x^2+y^2+2gx+2fy+c=0 \) is:
explanation: Length of tangent \( = \sqrt{S_1} = \sqrt{x_1^2+y_1^2+2gx_1+2fy_1+c} \)
name: Length of Tangent
subject: maths

text: The back-EMF in an electric motor is:
options: In the same direction as applied EMF
explanation: Back-EMF: EMF induced by rotating armature opposing applied EMF (Lenz's law). \( I = (V - \varepsilon_{back})/R \). At full speed, back-EMF approaches applied EMF.
name: Back-EMF in Motor
subject: phy

text: The point dividing \( P(x_1,y_1) \) and \( Q(x_2,y_2) \) in ratio \( m:n \) internally is:
explanation: Section Formula (internal): \( \left(\frac{mx_2+nx_1}{m+n}, \frac{my_2+ny_1}{m+n}\right) \)
name: Section Formula
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = \)
explanation: Power Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = nx^{n-1} \)
name: Power Rule
subject: maths

text: The Hall effect is used to determine:
options: Sign of charge carriers and carrier density: \( R_H = 1/(ne) \)
explanation: Hall effect: when current-carrying conductor is in \(\vec{B}\), transverse voltage (Hall voltage) develops. Determines sign of carriers and \( n = 1/(eR_H) \).
name: Hall Effect
subject: phy

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = a^x\ln a \). When \(a=e\): reduces to \(e^x\).
name: Derivative of aˣ
subject: maths

text: Gauss's law for magnetism states:
explanation: Gauss's law for magnetism: \( \oint \vec{B}\cdot d\vec{A} = 0 \). Magnetic field lines form closed loops (no magnetic monopoles exist).
name: Gauss's Law for Magnetism
subject: phy

text: The equivalent weight of \( \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \) (basicity = 2) is:
explanation: Equivalent weight \( = \frac{\text{Molar mass}}{n\text{-factor}} = \frac{98}{2} = 49 \text{ g/eq} \)
name: Equivalent Weight
subject: che

text: The moment of inertia of a ring about its diameter is:
explanation: Ring (axis through centre perpendicular): \( I = MR^2 \). Ring (diameter): \( I = \frac{MR^2}{2} \) (by perpendicular axis theorem: \( MR^2 = I_x + I_y = 2I_{diameter} \)).
name: MOI of Ring about Diameter
subject: phy

text: The number of tetrahedral voids per atom in a close-packed structure is:
explanation: In close packing: if number of atoms = N, octahedral voids = N, tetrahedral voids = 2N. So 2 tetrahedral voids per atom.
name: Tetrahedral Voids
subject: che

text: Polarisation of light proves that light is a:
options: Transverse wave (electric field oscillations perpendicular to propagation)
explanation: Polarisation is only possible for transverse waves. Light can be plane-polarised, showing it has transverse electric field oscillations.
name: Polarisation and Wave Nature
subject: phy

text: The internal energy of \(n\) moles of a monatomic ideal gas at temperature \(T\) is:
explanation: Monatomic ideal gas: 3 degrees of freedom. \( U = n \times \frac{3}{2}RT = \frac{3}{2}nRT \). For diatomic: \( U = \frac{5}{2}nRT \).
name: Internal Energy of Monatomic Gas
subject: phy

text: The normal form of a line is \( x\cos\alpha + y\sin\alpha = p \). Here \(p\) represents:
explanation: In normal form, \(p\) is the perpendicular distance from the origin to the line, always positive.
name: Normal Form
subject: maths

text: The forbidden energy gap (band gap) of silicon is approximately:
explanation: Band gaps: Ge ≈ 0.67 eV, Si ≈ 1.1 eV, GaAs ≈ 1.43 eV, ZnSe ≈ 3.2 eV. Conductors: 0 eV. Insulators: > 5 eV.
name: Band Gap of Silicon
subject: phy

text: The order of reaction is determined by:
options: The stoichiometry of the balanced equation
explanation: Order must be determined experimentally. It may or may not match stoichiometric coefficients.
name: Determining Reaction Order
subject: che

text: The area of parallelogram with adjacent sides \( \vec{a} \) and \( \vec{b} \) is:
explanation: Area of parallelogram \( = |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| \). Area of triangle \( = \frac{1}{2}|\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| \).
name: Area Using Cross Product
subject: maths

text: Bleaching powder contains:
explanation: Bleaching powder: Ca(OCl)Cl (mixed salt of Ca²⁺ with OCl⁻ and Cl⁻). Formula: CaOCl₂ or Ca(OCl)Cl. Made by Cl₂ + Ca(OH)₂.
name: Bleaching Powder
subject: che

text: From 6 men and 4 women, a committee of 3 men and 2 women can be formed in:
name: Committee Formation
subject: maths

text: The element with the highest first ionisation energy is:
explanation: Noble gases have highest IE in their period. He has the highest first IE of all elements (~2372 kJ/mol).
name: Highest Ionisation Energy
subject: che

text: A function \(f\) is increasing on an interval if:
explanation: \(f\) is increasing where \( f'(x) > 0 \) and decreasing where \( f'(x) < 0 \).
name: Increasing Function
subject: maths

text: The energy stored per unit volume in an electric field \(E\) is:
explanation: Electric field energy density: \( u = \frac{1}{2}\epsilon_0 E^2 \). Total energy in capacitor \( = u \times A d = \frac{1}{2}CV^2 \).
name: Electric Energy Density
subject: phy

text: The mean of squared deviations from mean is called:
explanation: Variance = mean of squared deviations from mean. SD is its square root.
name: Variance Definition
subject: maths

explanation: \( f(f(x)) = \frac{x-1}{x} \), \( f(f(f(x))) = \frac{1}{1-\frac{x-1}{x}} = x \). Period 3 composition.
name: Triple Composition
subject: maths

text: A reaction is spontaneous at all temperatures when:
explanation: \( \Delta G = \Delta H - T\Delta S \). When \(\Delta H < 0\) and \(\Delta S > 0\): \(\Delta G < 0\) always (at all T).
name: Spontaneous at All T
subject: che

text: The Gattermann reaction is a modification of Sandmeyer reaction using:
options: Cu powder (instead of CuX salt) + HX gas
explanation: Gattermann reaction: ArN₂⁺ + Cu powder + HX → ArX + N₂. Uses Cu metal + HX instead of CuX salt (Sandmeyer).
name: Gattermann Reaction
subject: che

text: If \( \vec{a} \perp \vec{b} \), then \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = \)
explanation: When \( \theta=90° \): \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\cos90° = 0 \)
name: Perpendicular Vectors
subject: maths

text: \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = \)
explanation: Definition of \(e\): \( \lim_{x\to\infty}\left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = e \approx 2.718 \)
name: Definition of e
subject: maths

text: The kinetic theory expression for pressure of gas is:
explanation: Kinetic theory: \( P = \frac{1}{3}\rho\overline{c^2} = \frac{1}{3}\frac{mN}{V}\overline{c^2} \)
name: Kinetic Theory Pressure
subject: che

text: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: FTC: \( \int_a^b f(x)dx = F(b)-F(a) \), where \(F'(x)=f(x)\).
name: Fundamental Theorem
subject: maths

text: The angle of minimum deviation \(D_m\) in a prism of apex angle \(A\) gives refractive index as:
explanation: Prism formula: \( n = \frac{\sin\frac{A+D_m}{2}}{\sin\frac{A}{2}} \). At minimum deviation, the ray passes symmetrically through the prism.
name: Prism Formula
subject: phy

text: The partial fraction form of \( \frac{2x+3}{(x^2+1)(x-2)} \) contains:
explanation: For an irreducible quadratic factor \(x^2+1\), the numerator in the partial fraction is linear: \(Ax+B\).
name: Irreducible Quadratic Factor
subject: maths

text: The standard equation of a hyperbola with transverse axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Hyperbola (transverse axis along x): \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \). Eccentricity \(e > 1\).
name: Hyperbola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: The power of a lens in dioptres (D) is:
explanation: Power: \( P = 1/f(\text{metres}) = 100/f(\text{cm}) \). Unit: dioptre (D). Converging lens: \(P > 0\). Diverging: \(P < 0\).
name: Power of Lens
subject: phy

text: If \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \), then the general solution is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \Rightarrow \tan\theta = 1 \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + \frac{\pi}{4} \)
name: sinθ = cosθ
subject: maths

text: The condition for \( lx+my+n=0 \) to be tangent to ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: Tangent condition to ellipse: \( a^2l^2+b^2m^2=n^2 \)
name: Tangent Condition to Ellipse
subject: maths

text: Raoult's law states that the vapour pressure of a solution is:
explanation: Raoult's Law: \( P = x_A P^\circ_A + x_B P^\circ_B \). For non-volatile solute: \( P = x_A P^\circ_A \).
name: Raoult's Law
subject: che

text: Bayes' theorem states: \( P(A_i|B) = \)
explanation: Bayes' theorem: \( P(A_i|B) = \frac{P(A_i)P(B|A_i)}{\sum_j P(A_j)P(B|A_j)} \)
name: Bayes' Theorem
subject: maths

text: The stability of chelate complexes compared to analogous non-chelate complexes is known as:
explanation: Chelate effect: chelate complexes are thermodynamically more stable than analogous non-chelate complexes due to increased entropy and ring formation.
name: Chelate Effect
subject: che

text: If \( AB = BA = I \), then \( B \) is called:
explanation: By definition, if \( AB = BA = I \), then \( B = A^{-1} \), the inverse of \(A\).
name: Matrix Inverse Definition
subject: maths
